children.label: i
children.text: the consortium coordinated by Mt. Sinai Hospital in New York City that coordinates the monitoring and treatment for enrolled WTC responders other than with respect to those covered under the arrangement with the Fire Department of New York City; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the Fire Department of New York City.

children.heading: Responder who meets current eligibility criteria
children.label: B Responder who meets current eligibility criteria
children.text: An individual who meets the current eligibility criteria described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Responder who meets modified eligibility criteria
children.label: C Responder who meets modified eligibility criteria
i
children.text: performed rescue, recovery, demolition, debris cleanup, or other related services in the New York City disaster area in response to the , terrorist attacks, regardless of whether such services were performed by a State or Federal employee or member of the National Guard or otherwise; andSeptember 11, 2001

children.label: ii
children.text: meets such eligibility criteria relating to exposure to airborne toxins, other hazards, or adverse conditions resulting from the , terrorist attacks as the WTC Program Administrator, after consultation with the WTC Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee, determines appropriate.September 11, 2001

children.label: i
children.text: was a member of the Fire Department of New York City (whether fire or emergency personnel, active or retired) who participated at least one day in the rescue and recovery effort at any of the former World Trade Center sites (including Ground Zero, Staten Island Landfill, and the New York City Chief Medical Examiner’s Office) for any time during the period beginning on , and ending on ; orSeptember 11, 2001July 31, 2002

children.label: I
children.text: is a surviving immediate family member of an individual who was a member of the Fire Department of New York City (whether fire or emergency personnel, active or retired) and was killed at the World Trade site on ; andSeptember 11, 2001

children.label: II
children.text: received any treatment for a WTC-related health condition described in (relating to mental health conditions) on or before .section 300mm–22(a)(1)(A)(ii) of this titleSeptember 1, 2008

children.heading: Law enforcement officers and WTC rescue, recovery, and cleanup workers
children.label: B Law enforcement officers and WTC rescue, recovery, and cleanup workers
i
children.text: worked or volunteered onsite in rescue, recovery, debris cleanup, or related support services in lower Manhattan (south of Canal St.), the Staten Island Landfill, or the barge loading piers, for at least 4 hours during the period beginning on , and ending on , for at least 24 hours during the period beginning on , and ending on , or for at least 80 hours during the period beginning on , and ending on ;September 11, 2001September 14, 2001September 11, 2001September 30, 2001September 11, 2001July 31, 2002

children.label: I
children.text: was a member of the Police Department of New York City (whether active or retired) or a member of the Port Authority Police of the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey (whether active or retired) who participated onsite in rescue, recovery, debris cleanup, or related services in lower Manhattan (south of Canal St.), including Ground Zero, the Staten Island Landfill, or the barge loading piers, for at least 4 hours during the period beginning , and ending on ;September 11, 2001September 14, 2001

children.label: II
children.text: participated onsite in rescue, recovery, debris cleanup, or related services at Ground Zero, the Staten Island Landfill, or the barge loading piers, for at least one day during the period beginning on , and ending on ;September 11, 2001July 31, 2002

children.label: III
children.text: participated onsite in rescue, recovery, debris cleanup, or related services in lower Manhattan (south of Canal St.) for at least 24 hours during the period beginning on , and ending on ; orSeptember 11, 2001September 30, 2001

children.label: IV
children.text: participated onsite in rescue, recovery, debris cleanup, or related services in lower Manhattan (south of Canal St.) for at least 80 hours during the period beginning on , and ending on ;September 11, 2001July 31, 2002

children.label: iii
children.text: was an employee of the Office of the Chief Medical Examiner of New York City involved in the examination and handling of human remains from the World Trade Center attacks, or other morgue worker who performed similar post-September 11 functions for such Office staff, during the period beginning on , and ending on ;September 11, 2001July 31, 2002

children.label: iv
children.text: was a worker in the Port Authority Trans-Hudson Corporation Tunnel for at least 24 hours during the period beginning on , and ending on ; orFebruary 1, 2002July 1, 2002

children.label: v
children.text: was a vehicle-maintenance worker who was exposed to debris from the former World Trade Center while retrieving, driving, cleaning, repairing, and maintaining vehicles contaminated by airborne toxins from the , terrorist attacks during a duration and period described in subparagraph (A).September 11, 2001

children.heading: Responders to the September 11 attacks at the Pentagon and Shanksville, Pennsylvania
children.label: C Responders to the September 11 attacks at the Pentagon and Shanksville, Pennsylvania
I
children.text: was a member of a fire or police department (whether fire or emergency personnel, active or retired), worked for a recovery or cleanup contractor, or was a volunteer; and performed rescue, recovery, demolition, debris cleanup, or other related services at the Pentagon site of the terrorist-related aircraft crash of , during the period beginning on , and ending on the date on which the cleanup of the site was concluded, as determined by the WTC Program Administrator;September 11, 2001September 11, 2001

children.label: II
children.text: was a member of a fire or police department (whether fire or emergency personnel, active or retired), worked for a recovery or cleanup contractor, or was a volunteer; and performed rescue, recovery, demolition, debris cleanup, or other related services at the Shanksville, Pennsylvania, site of the terrorist-related aircraft crash of , during the period beginning on , and ending on the date on which the cleanup of the site was concluded, as determined by the WTC Program Administrator;September 11, 2001September 11, 2001

children.label: III
children.text: was an employee of the Department of Defense or any other Federal agency, worked during the period beginning on , and ending on , for a contractor of the Department of Defense or any other Federal agency, or was a member of a regular or reserve component of the uniformed services; and performed rescue, recovery, demolition, debris cleanup, or other related services at the Pentagon site of the terrorist-related aircraft crash of , during the period beginning on , and ending on the date on which the cleanup of the site was concluded, as determined by the WTC Program Administrator; orSeptember 11, 2001September 18, 2001September 11, 2001September 11, 2001

children.label: IV
children.text: was an employee of the Department of Defense or any other Federal agency, worked during the period beginning on , and ending on , for a contractor of the Department of Defense or any other Federal agency, or was a member of a regular or reserve component of the uniformed services; and performed rescue, recovery, demolition, debris cleanup, or other related services at the Shanksville, Pennsylvania, site of the terrorist-related aircraft crash of , during the period beginning on , and ending on the date on which the cleanup of the site was concluded, as determined by the WTC Program Administrator; andSeptember 11, 2001September 18, 2001September 11, 2001September 11, 2001

children.label: ii
children.text: is determined by the WTC Program Administrator to be at an increased risk of developing a WTC-related health condition as a result of exposure to airborne toxins, other hazards, or adverse conditions resulting from the , terrorist attacks, and meets such eligibility criteria related to such exposures, as the WTC Program Administrator determines are appropriate, after consultation with the WTC Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee.September 11, 2001

children.label: i
children.text: WTC responders described in paragraph (1)(A) shall be deemed to be enrolled in such Program;

children.label: ii
children.text: subject to clause (iii), the Administrator shall enroll in such program individuals who are determined to be WTC responders;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Administrator shall deny such enrollment to an individual if the Administrator determines that the numerical limitation in paragraph (4) on enrollment of WTC responders has been met;

children.label: iv
children.text: there shall be no fee charged to the applicant for making an application for such enrollment;

children.label: v
children.text: the Administrator shall make a determination on such an application not later than 60 days after the date of filing the application; and

children.label: vi
children.text: an individual who is denied enrollment in such Program shall have an opportunity to appeal such determination in a manner established under such process.

children.heading: Currently identified responders
children.label: i Currently identified responders
children.text: In accordance with subparagraph (A)(i), the WTC Program Administrator shall enroll an individual described in paragraph (1)(A) in the WTC Program not later than .July 1, 2011

children.heading: Other responders
children.label: ii Other responders
children.text: In accordance with subparagraph (A)(ii) and consistent with paragraph (4), the WTC Program Administrator shall enroll any other individual who is determined to be a WTC responder in the WTC Program at the time of such determination.

children.heading: Numerical limitation on eligible WTC responders
children.label: 4 Numerical limitation on eligible WTC responders

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The total number of individuals not described in paragraph (1)(A) or (2)(A)(ii) or subclause (III) or (IV) of paragraph (2)(C)(i) who may be enrolled under paragraph (3)(A)(ii) shall not exceed 75,000 at any time, of which no more than 2,500 may be individuals enrolled based on modified eligibility criteria established under paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: Certain responders to the September 11 attacks at the Pentagon and Shanksville, Pennsylvania
children.label: ii Certain responders to the September 11 attacks at the Pentagon and Shanksville, Pennsylvania
children.text: The total number of individuals who may be enrolled under paragraph (3)(A)(ii) based on eligibility criteria described in subclause (III) or (IV) of paragraph (2)(C)(i) shall not exceed 500 at any time.

children.label: I
children.text: in accordance with such subparagraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: to such number, as determined by the Administrator based on the best available information and subject to amounts available under this subchapter, that will ensure sufficient funds will be available to provide treatment and monitoring benefits under this subchapter, with respect to all individuals who are enrolled; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide priority (subject to paragraph (3)(A)(i)) in such enrollments in the order in which individuals apply for enrollment under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Disqualification of individuals on terrorist watch list
children.label: 5 Disqualification of individuals on terrorist watch list
children.text: No individual who is on the terrorist watch list maintained by the Department of Homeland Security shall qualify as an eligible WTC responder. Before enrolling any individual as a WTC responder in the WTC Program under paragraph (3), the Administrator, in consultation with the Secretary of Homeland Security, shall determine whether the individual is on such list.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of an enrolled WTC responder (other than one described in subsection (a)(2)(A)(ii)), the WTC Program shall provide for monitoring benefits that include monitoring consistent with protocols approved by the WTC Program Administrator and including clinical examinations and long-term health monitoring and analysis. In the case of an enrolled WTC responder who is an active member of the Fire Department of New York City, the responder shall receive such benefits as part of the individual’s periodic company medical exams.

children.heading: Provision of monitoring benefits
children.label: 2 Provision of monitoring benefits
children.text: The monitoring benefits under paragraph (1) shall be provided through the Clinical Center of Excellence for the type of individual involved or, in the case of an individual residing outside the New York metropolitan area, under an arrangement under .section 300mm–23 of this title
